title: Deliciously Spiced Moroccan Lamb Briouat
description: A cultured and festive Moroccan dish featuring lamb and five spice powder, perfect for special occasions. This flexitarian-friendly recipe is boiled and then fried to create a crispy and molten treat.
instructions: In a large pot, boil the lamb shoulder in water until it is tender, then drain and set aside.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the onion and garlic until they are softened.
Add the five spice powder, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the cooked lamb to the skillet and stir to combine with the spice mixture.
Lay a warqa dough sheet on a flat surface and place a tablespoon of the lamb mixture in the center of the dough.
Brush the edges of the dough with water and fold the dough into a triangle, pressing the edges to seal.
Repeat with the remaining dough and filling.
Heat about 2 inches of oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat and fry the briouat until they are golden brown.
Drain the briouat on paper towels and serve hot.
